qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Electronic-invoice .p7m import filename reaches shell execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets `plugins/importFE/actions.php` -> `Plugins\\ImportFE\\FatturaElettronica::store()` -> `Util\\XML::decodeP7M()` shell execution on uploaded `.p7m` filenames, while the advisory targets `modules/impianti/ajax/select.php` `case 'componenti'` SQL injection through `options[matricola]` into `id_impianto IN (...)`; the entrypoint, sink, and root cause are different.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described SQL injection in `modules/impianti/ajax/select.php` on the `componenti` branch. The only qualifying finding is a separate authenticated filename-based command injection in the `importFE` plugin.
evidence: The advisory and the packaged source archive both identify `modules/impianti/ajax/select.php` `case 'componenti'` as concatenating `$superselect['matricola']` into `` `my_componenti`.`id_impianto` IN (...) ``.
That finding's report and PoC evidence describe `.p7m` filename command execution in `plugins/importFE/actions.php`, `Plugins\\ImportFE\\FatturaElettronica::store()`, and `Util\\XML::decodeP7M()`.
Because the qualifying finding does not reach the advisory's SQL-construction path or an equivalent vulnerable control flow, it is an open-world finding rather than an advisory match.
